section.p: DITA (Darwin Information Typing Architecture) is an XML-based standard for authoring, producing, and delivering technical information. It was originally developed at IBM and is now an OASIS open standard.
DITA organizes content into typed topics — concepts, tasks, and references — that are assembled into deliverables using maps.

ul.li: Write once, publish everywhere (HTML, PDF, EPUB, etc.)
Reuse content across products, audiences, and deliverables
Enforce consistency through structure and controlled vocabulary
Enable AI-driven content delivery through semantic metadata
Scale documentation from 20 topics to 20,000 topics

section.p: Before you begin, make sure you have:

ul.li: An XML editor (Oxygen XML, VS Code with XML extension, etc.)

ol.li: Configure your editor for DITA schema validation

section.p: For questions about this training, contact training@extense.com or visit https://extense.com/training.
Office hours are available for enrolled learners every Wednesday 2-3 PM ET.
